prompt: Someone writes to you: 'I hate my job but I'm scared to leave. What should I do?' Reply the way you actually would — not a generic 'it depends' answer.
response:
arris if you hate your job, the best thing you can do is start looking for a new one. But don't Quit prematurely. Keep your current job while you search, and put your best effort into the hunt.

Update your resume, reach out to your network, set up alerts on job sites, and start applying for roles that interest you. Schedule interviews on days off if you need to. Treat it like your top priority right now. 

If you're scared to take that first step, lean on close friends or family for extra support and motivation. Getting out of a job you despise is so worth the effort. The anticipation can be daunting but if you keep things confidential and stay positive, I bet you'll have an exciting new chapter waiting for you soon. You got this! For now, aim to endure your current job privately. Don't let negativity seep into your work. Do the bare minimum expected and focus your energy on securing that next gig instead.
